CREATE TABLE employees (id BIGINT, name VARCHAR, department VARCHAR, salary BIGINT);
SELECT name, salary, rank_dense() OVER (PARTITION BY department ORDER BY salary DESC) as rank FROM employees;
CREATE TABLE projects (project_id BIGINT, employee_id BIGINT, project_name VARCHAR);
SELECT e.name, e.salary, p.project_name, rank_dense() OVER (PARTITION BY e.department ORDER BY e.salary DESC) as rank FROM employees e JOIN projects p ON e.id = p.employee_id;
SELECT department, AVG(salary) as avg_salary, rank_dense() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(salary) DESC) as rank FROM employees GROUP BY department;
SELECT e.name, e.salary, rank_dense() OVER (ORDER BY e.salary DESC) as rank FROM (SELECT id, name, salary FROM employees WHERE salary > 65000) AS e;
CREATE TABLE sales (id BIGINT, employee_id BIGINT, sale_amount BIGINT);
SELECT e.name, s.sale_amount, rank_dense() OVER (PARTITION BY e.department ORDER BY s.sale_amount DESC) as rank FROM employees e JOIN sales s ON e.id = s.employee_id;
SELECT e.department, e.salary, rank_dense() OVER (PARTITION BY e.department ORDER BY e.salary ASC) as rank FROM (SELECT * FROM employees WHERE name LIKE 'A%') AS e;
